statement: Do not create standalone excerpts that add no real benefit and only waste focus.
what_it_prevents: Useless micro-entries that subtract from study quality.

statement: Do not use shortness alone as a justification for excerpting or rejection.
Do not treat harmlessness and usefulness as the same thing.
what_it_prevents: False promotion of weak segments into strong standalone entries.

statement: Do not let subjective preference for long or short excerpts control objective excerpting.
Do not strip harmless structural transition text if retaining it inside adjacent excerpts adds value.
what_it_prevents: Loss of structural continuity where retention is cheap and useful.

statement: Do not confuse retained-context value or duplication value with strong standalone value.
what_it_prevents: Inflated claims about what a micro-excerpt accomplishes on its own.

statement: Do not flatten explicit owner tension into a clean answer that erases one side of the reaction.
Do not assume every short structural fragment deserves duplication by default; duplication must also clear the harmlessness and added-benefit test.
